page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-24GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 24.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.0 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, requiring external storage attachments for persistent data.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile with distinct tradeoffs. The shared CPU allocation results in poor single-core performance, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 772.79, which falls into the bottom 10 percent of tested servers. However, multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6254.7 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 6189.0. Memory performance is mixed; while cached read-only small block bandwidth is average at 821.2 GB/sec, the PassMark memory score is weak, placing in the bottom 25 percent. Static web serving throughput is also weak at 6.02 GB/sec.

This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads that do not require sustained peak single-core performance. It is suited for tasks such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate database operations, as evidenced by an average PassMark database score of 4451.22 and 11.84 million Redis SET operations per second. It is less suitable for large-scale LLM inference or high-throughput web serving due to weak performance in those specific areas.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 24.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a significant tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, falling in the bottom 10 percent, whereas multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth generally align with average mid-range standards. PassMark memory scores and static web serving throughput are weak, but the server demonstrates solid multi-threaded decompression and database operation metrics. This configuration is qualitatively cost-effective for general-purpose, multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and moderate database tasks that do not demand dedicated CPU cores or high single-thread performance.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 24 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance with poor single-core speeds and no local storage.

tagline: A shared-core virtual server balancing eight AMD EPYC vCPUs and twenty-four gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded general-purpose workloads.

24.0 GB of system memory with no bundled local storage
Average multi-core performance alongside weak single-core speeds
Suited for software compilation and multi-threaded decompression
Capable of moderate database operations and Redis caching
